title: MQL5 Asian Breakout EA (article 17239): same strategy family as Trellis v4 — session box + MA filter + fixed RR; numbers only in screenshots
claim: A published MQL5 EA implements the same architecture as our system — Asian session box (23:00-03:00 GMT high/low), stop orders beyond the box, 50-period MA trend filter, SL outside the box, TP at 1.3x risk, time exit 13:00 — and reports a 2023 backtest whose equity improved after adding a trailing stop; no numeric results appear in the article text (screenshots only).

source.label: MQL5.com — Automating Trading Strategies in MQL5 (Part 9): Building an Expert Advisor for the Asian Breakout Strategy
source.quote: place a Buy Stop order slightly above the top of the box ... a sell-stop order just below the bottom of the box ... [50-period MA trend filter; session 23:00-03:00 GMT; BreakoutOffsetPips = 10; TP multiplier 1.3; trade exit 13:00]

rule_formal: Box = [min(Low), max(High)] over 23:00-03:00 GMT. BuyStop = BoxHigh + 10 pips if Price > SMA(50); SellStop = BoxLow - 10 pips if Price < SMA(50). SL just outside opposite box boundary; TP = Entry + (Entry - SL) * 1.3. Force-exit 13:00 GMT.
mechanism: Asian-session consolidation defines resting liquidity beyond both extremes; London/NY participation resolves the box directionally.
relevance: Comparative design point for the whole system (their box window 23:00-03:00 GMT is narrower than our 01-08 EET; RR fixed 1.3 vs our 1xR trail; exit 13:00 vs our EOD 23:00) — differences are testable knobs; trailing-stop-improves claim touches Q3

test_design.lookahead_risk: none — box complete before entry window

overlap_risk: not a new variable — an alternative parameterization of the existing system (window/RR/exit-time); no overlap with falsified day-selection variables
